title: Burns (starter evidence summary)
text:
Telephone burn triage focuses on burn depth, size, special locations, and inhalation injury.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Smoke inhalation concerns: trouble breathing, hoarseness, soot exposure, facial burns in a fire

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Deep burns (white/charred, numb areas), large blistering
- Burns on face, hands, genitals, or over major joints
- Large area burns (bigger than the patient's palm) or worsening pain/redness

Supportive care (minor burns):
- Cool running water (not ice), keep clean, cover lightly.
- Seek in-person care if worsening pain, spreading redness, fever, or pus develops.
